entities.name: Bad Self-Advocates Frame
entities.context: Voice primitive reframing candidate rejection as a failure of self-advocacy detection, not candidate quality

entities.name: Conceptual Frame Pattern
entities.context: Rhetorical pattern: 'You're not rejecting [X], you're rejecting [Y which is a subset of X]' — used to correct misdiagnoses

entities.name: Hiring
entities.context: Domain where the bad self-advocates frame applies — reframing how managers evaluate candidates

entities.name: Talent
entities.context: Domain where the bad self-advocates frame applies — distinguishing talent from self-presentation skill

entities.name: Leadership
entities.context: Domain where the bad self-advocates frame applies — correcting leader assumptions about rejection decisions

entities.name: LinkedIn
entities.context: Source platform where V published or developed this voice primitive

entities.name: Vrijen Attawar
entities.context: Author of the voice primitive; founder of Careerspan, career coach

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company — the hiring/talent context of this primitive aligns with Careerspan's mission

relationships.context: Core V belief: rejection in hiring is misdirected at candidate quality when the real issue is self-advocacy skill
This primitive uses the 'not X, actually Y' reframing rhetorical structure
Primary domain of application — correcting how hiring managers interpret candidate performance
Challenges the conflation of talent with ability to self-advocate
Targets leaders who misdiagnose rejection decisions as quality filtering
Platform where V sourced or published this primitive
Directly supports Careerspan's value proposition — surfacing talent that traditional hiring misses
